desc:
The earth opens up into a large black hallway, dominated by a few protruding
boulders and a somewhat smooth rock floor. Though much of this area may have
been a natural formation in the earth, its looks as though at least some of the
rock has been broken and formed into the shape of a large entryway. A thick
white mist sits subdued on the ground, stirred only by the occasional quick
movement of an unseen, low-lying creature. An atmosphere of evil lurks here with
a foreboding presence; it waits in the cracks and crevices to instil fear in any
unwelcome visitors.
name: Disturbed, Stone-laden Hallway

desc:
This was once a well-crafted passage. The walls are made of smooth slabs of grey
stone, but time has not been kind to them. Many are cracked and a few lie
shattered on the ground, showing the black basalt rock behind.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
This passageway continues to both the east and west. The walls are well-made and
clad in slabs of grey stone. The walls encroach on the passageway on both sides,
and the hall is three times as high as it is wide.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
Ages old building material stands stacked against the walls in this chamber.
Everything is caked in dust and it is clear nothing here has been used for a
very long time. Most of the material is slabs of grey stone, seemingly the same
material used for the walls here.
name: Disused Storage Chamber

desc:
Dark and narrow, this underground passageway connects to a larger chamber to the
east. Dust has gathered on the floor and many of the slabs the walls are made of
are cracked.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
A layer of dust cakes everything in this large, open chamber. The chamber is
mostly empty, except for several statues that stand ready, clad in stone armour
with their swords drawn. Some of the statues have crumbled completely and lie on
the floor in piles of rubble, others are missing limbs.
name: Watchful Hall

desc:
The ledge is clear of any rubble or debris, with a few skeletons decaying along
edges of a cavern entrance. The ledge leads to the east, where the entrance
almost appears to be yawning widely. The ledge leading down is steep and it is
hard to judge the distance below.
name: Precarious Ledge

desc:
Large slabs of rock and granite have fallen from above and formed gaping cracks
and crevices in the cave floor. A small, murky pool of water has formed beneath
where some of the slabs have punched through. A green film forms a slight sheen
on the surface of the water, and a green slime has accumulated around its edges.
Occasional ripples can be seen on the surface.
name: Partially-collapsed Cavern

desc:
The cave to the north leads to this small niche near the murky pool. The water
is dark and clouded deeper within the pool. Near the edges the stone of the
cavern is more visible. Moss grows heavily along the stone walls here; bright
green near edge of the water and darker green toward the western side of the
pool. The ground is damp and humidity fills the air.
name: Niche by a Pool

desc:
The cave is expansive to the north and east, with moss growing in patches along
the wall. Cobwebs hang in several places, most appearing to be abandoned long
ago. Almost the entire southern wall of the cave is covered by webs both old and
new, with natural debris being trapped within. The cave opens up along the
western side where a ledge appears to be; the ground slightly inclined and less
damp as it leads to the ledge.
name: Murky Cave

desc:
The cavern walls are black basalt to the south and east, connecting to a low
ceiling that is both rough and uneven. The ground is rough and dark like the
walls, with dying moss scattered along the ground in patches. Humidity and
dampness can be felt towards the northern area of the cave here and the air
appears less thick toward the west.
name: Corner of a Murky Cave

desc:
The dark cave expands in every direction other than the east, where there must
have been a large cave-in recently, judging from the dust still settling and the
worn path that disappears under the rock and debris. The black basalt rubble
from the cave-in looks to be impossible to clear.
name: Murky Cave

desc:
The air is damp within the cave here and it becomes quite thick towards the
eastern side. Although it is dark and murky, there are small patches of moss
growing throughout the cave; the moss is a deep green and appears to be thriving
on the humidity and darkness here. The walls of the cave expand to the west and
south, and a slow drip of water can be heard echoing off the cavern walls to the
east.
name: Murky Cave

desc:
The dark ground is damp from humidity throughout the murky cave, which continues
to both the south and east. The cavern wall rises beyond sight to the north and
west, with moss growing from the tiny cracks and crevices along the base of the
walls. The moss is a deep green in patches along the edges of the cave, making
it appear as if a worn path was formed along the ground from the southern and
eastern areas of the cave.
name: Murky Cave

desc:
The murky and dark cave is wide here, continuing in all directions other than
the west, where the cavern wall rises beyond sight into a deeper darkness. Moss
grows along the western wall, rooting into the rocks and crevices where it can.
name: Murky Cave

desc:
The cave to the south widens here at a long and steep ledge. The damp and murky
air from the southern cave lingers here, and a slight draft can be felt leading
downward beyond the ledge and out of sight. Moss grows along the mouth of the
cave to the south, but the ledge itself is bare and the ground is dark. Some of
the ledge appears to be worn, as if often used to reach whatever is below.
name: Damp Ledge

desc:
This lair has only one way out and that is to the west, where the black basalt
walls are covered by fresh and old webs. Several cocoons are suspended within
the webbing, with dry corpses decaying inside them. Although it is too high to
reach, there are many holes in the ceiling above that appear to be tunnels that
twist beyond sight. Moss growing along the base of the cavern walls that lead to
the mass of cobwebs to the west is drying out and slowly dying. Cracked shells
and other debris are scattered throughout the lair.
name: Foul-smelling Lair

desc:
A chamber with walls of slabs of grey rock, well crafted but old. A passage to
the west exits the chamber, and there is another opening to the north. A huge
skull has been forged into the walls around the northern opening, from the look
of it a monstrous wyrm.
name: Dusty Chamber

desc:
A vast cavern of epic proportions expands endlessly to the east and north. The
walls, if they even exist, are shrouded in veils of darkness and gloom. The
floor of the cavern is smooth but littered with old, crumbled items. Here and
there, something shiny glitters amongst the garbage.
name: Dragon's Hoard

desc:
The walls are covered with the scratches of giant claws. The ceiling so high
it's lost in darkness and can't be seen. In one place, there are some narrow but
very deep cracks in the cavern's floor where the rock has split apart. A faint
sulphurous stench wafts up from the unknown depths.
name: Vast Dragon's Hoard

desc:
Once, these vast caves extended to the north and west. In a past age, the cave
must have collapsed, for a large cave-in blocks the way. The floor is mostly
smooth, aside from where rents of long deep grooves mar it. Scattered across the
ground are piles of what might have been treasure once upon a time, though it
has long since mostly rotted, rusted, and crumbled.
name: Dark Corner in a Dragon's Hoard

desc:
A large cavern expands to the west and south from this dark corner. Black rock
make up the walls and ceiling, and most likely the ground as well although it is
covered by millennia of dust and dirt. There is also an enormous amount of old,
decayed junk gathered in piles.
name: Far Corner in a Dragon's Hoard

desc:
A slight depression in the floor here, the ground is slightly lower than the
surrounding caves. As a result, extra much dust and rubbage have gathered here.
The eastern wall is scored by deep gouge marks, it looks almost like an enormous
creature had a hissy fit here.
name: Vast Dragon's Hoard

desc:
Ancient items litter this cavern. Chests, ancient relics, glittering things
resting amongst piles of rotted cloth, these halls seems to hide an unimaginable
wealth. All of it appearingly unguarded, for a layer of dust coates everything.
name: Treasure-filled part of a Dragon's Hoard
